gates.title: Publication Inventory Complete
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG0 is the evidence-based exit gate from P0 into full Architectural Foundations drafting. It remains unpassed until the approved inventory, identifier, evidence, terminology, scope, classification, gap, and contradiction conditions are verified.
public_pass_criteria: Every completed phase has a catalog entry.
ADR-IMP-001 through ADR-IMP-029 are accounted for.
Requirements, principles, invariants, gates, and spikes have stable identifiers.
Terminology conflicts have canonical resolutions and retained aliases.
Every normative item has approval evidence.
Deferred and conditional capabilities are identified.
Public/private classification is recorded.
Gaps and potential contradictions are visible.
No unresolved gap prevents accurate drafting of Architectural Foundations.
No genuine contradiction has been concealed through editorial normalization.

gates.title: Architectural Foundations Approved
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: all chapters are drafted from approved or properly cited sources.
historical narrative and normative decisions are distinguishable.
the companion-intelligence framing is understandable without the Blueprint.
the relationship between AI and DIP avoids model-as-authority ambiguity.
the first-domain story does not narrow the platform’s governing purpose.
no private Workspace or Private Strategy information is exposed.
every architectural summary links to its normative source.
deferred capabilities are not presented as implemented.
unsupported novelty or market claims have been removed or qualified.
terminology matches the P0 register.
Word and PDF renderings pass visual review.

gates.title: Living Architecture Blueprint Approved
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG2 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: all 18 modules are complete.
every approved architecture area is represented.
all normative requirements have stable identifiers.
all implementation ADRs are linked.
logical architecture and initial deployment are clearly distinguished.
initial, conditional, deferred, and portability statuses are complete.
diagrams agree with prose and contracts.
public examples use synthetic fixtures.
no private authority content is exposed.
contradiction and gap logs contain no unresolved publication blocker.
conformance profiles and evidence requirements are complete.
cross-reference validation passes.
Word and PDF editions pass visual review.
the Blueprint can direct implementation without requiring engineers to recover decisions from prior conversations.

gates.title: ADR and Decision Register Approved
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: all known ADRs are inventoried.
ADR-IMP-001 through ADR-IMP-029 are present and correctly titled.
existing identifiers are preserved.
non-ADR approved decisions have stable decision identifiers.
every retrospective record has evidence and reconstruction metadata.
accepted decisions are separated from unapproved recommendations.
lifecycle and implementation statuses are distinct.
supersession relationships are complete.
every governing decision links to the Blueprint.
every normative Blueprint area links back to governing decisions.
public records contain no private evidence or Workspace data.
the private evidence ledger is complete enough to support future audit.
consolidated Markdown, JSON, and CSV registers generate successfully.
Architecture v1.0 can pin the exact decision corpus.

gates.title: Architecture v1.0 Released
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: PG0 through PG3 have passed.
every required release component is included.
all six review tracks are complete.
no unresolved contradiction affects governing behavior.
all known limitations are classified and disclosed.
the Architect’s Letter is approved.
the release manifest identifies every artifact.
checksums verify.
Public-reference and restricted-stewardship editions contain only appropriately classified material; live Private Authority remains external and Secrets are excluded.
Word and PDF editions pass visual review.
the release is pinned to an immutable source revision.
the human approval record is complete.
the implementation kickoff package can reference the release without ambiguity.

gates.title: Publication Package Reproducible and Archived
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG5 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: all canonical sources are editable and complete.
the build works from a clean environment.
Public-reference and restricted-stewardship build separation passes; live Private Authority remains external and Secrets remain excluded.
all structural validations pass.
all DOCX and PDF pages have been visually reviewed.
modular and compiled editions agree semantically.
manifests identify every release file.
checksums validate.
archives extract successfully.
archive recovery testing passes.
README-FIRST instructions are complete.
generation dependencies and versions are recorded.
no generated file has become an independent source of truth.
the package remains understandable without access to the original conversations.
The public edition is proven independent of restricted sources.
The restricted stewardship edition is access-controlled.

gates.title: Implementation Kickoff Package Approved
gates.normalized_publication_statement: PG6 is formally evaluable but remains unpassed until every approved criterion is produced, verified, and approved.
public_pass_criteria: the kickoff manifest pins Architecture v1.0.
every authorized work item traces to approved architecture.
every work item has acceptance criteria and evidence expectations.
initial and excluded scope are unambiguous.
public, restricted, private-authority, and secret boundaries are represented.
build-gate dependencies are complete.
the first vertical slice has an end-to-end completion definition.
contradiction and scope-change procedures are approved.
the public package contains no private operational information.
restricted implementation material is access-controlled.
the human implementation-start checklist is ready.

gates.title: Implementation Authorized
gates.normalized_publication_statement: DIP product/runtime implementation remains prohibited until a separate explicit human approval event satisfies every IKG0 prerequisite.
public_pass_criteria: PG0 through PG5 passed.
P6 package approved.
Architecture v1.0 released.
kickoff manifest pinned.
human authorization recorded.
public and restricted work locations established.

gates.title: Production Launch Readiness Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Production launch shall remain prohibited until CI and activation, release identity, migrations, isolation, security, key and secret operation, backup and restore, incident response, operational conformance, cost controls, model qualification, MCP disposition, external-write state, and critical-failure clearance are evidenced and approved.

gates.title: Remote Production Security Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Remote production exposure shall remain prohibited until identity, session, authorization, RLS, isolation, encryption, secret, audit, network-edge, threat-model, ASVS-targeted, step-up, and fail-closed controls are evidenced and approved.

gates.title: Published Architecture Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Published Architecture Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: Implementation Planning Section 12 is approved.
The ADR ledger is reconciled.
Required publication artifacts are complete.
No unresolved contradiction exists among the locked architecture, AI governance, Constitution, and Implementation Planning.

gates.title: Foundation Conformance Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Foundation Conformance Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: Repository bootstrap succeeds from documented instructions.
Strict typing and required tests pass.
Migrations apply to an empty database.
Configuration bundles compile and activate atomically.
Private Authority and Secrets remain outside the public repository.
Workflow retry and recovery tests pass.

gates.title: First Vertical Slice Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: First Vertical Slice Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: The first Workspace refresh traverses the entire lifecycle.
Every canonical fact has accepted-artifact lineage.
Deterministic findings and the governed AI brief are reproducible from pinned inputs.
The Review Service receives the result.
At least one internal Decision, Command, and Outcome path is demonstrated.
Duplicate triggers do not create duplicate semantic effects.
Failure injection and recovery pass.

gates.title: First Workspace Usefulness Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: First Workspace Usefulness Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: Recurring refresh and available historical backfill operate.
Core first-domain entity, transaction, and change intelligence is useful.
Market-intelligence qualification requirements pass.
Value-dependent analyzers abstain when evidence is unavailable.
Public Workspace and Private Strategy analysis remain separated.

gates.title: Interaction Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Interaction Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: MCP capability is verified and activated or explicitly deferred.
The Operations Page supports launch-critical actions.
Every active interface calls the same application services.
Stable deep links and actor attribution work.

gates.title: Portability Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Portability Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: The second Workspace activates through configuration and Domain Package registration.
Its identity crosswalk and source data remain private.
Synthetic equivalents cover public contract testing.
No platform-core branching is required.
Workspace isolation tests pass in both directions.

gates.title: Production Readiness Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Production Readiness Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: Production deployment is reproducible by immutable image digest.
Migrations and activation procedures are rehearsed.
Backup and restore objectives are demonstrated.
Threat-model and security controls pass.
Operational alerts and runbooks exist.
Actual monthly cost fits the approved envelope or has explicit approval.
The formal Production Readiness Assessment is accepted.

gates.title: Market Intelligence Qualification Gate
gates.normalized_publication_statement: Market Intelligence Qualification Gate remains not passed until every normalized criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: At least one qualified automated market-value source is operational.
At least one additional automated or imported comparison source is available.
Provenance, observation time, entity identity, format, and source terms are recorded.
Stale, missing, and conflicting valuations are explicit.
Source acquisition is compliant and operationally supportable.
Analyzers abstain when evidence is inadequate.
Model intuition is never substituted silently for missing market data.

gates.title: Engineering Foundation Ready
gates.normalized_publication_statement: IKG1 remains not passed until every approved criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: repository structure.
uv workspace.
Python runtime.
linting and formatting.
strict type checking.
test execution.
contract validation.
public/private source separation.
secret detection.
clean-build reproducibility.

gates.title: Contract and Persistence Foundation Ready
gates.normalized_publication_statement: IKG2 remains not passed until every approved criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: authoritative schemas.
Pydantic boundaries.
database migrations.
artifact and version identities.
payload addressing.
provenance and lineage.
Unit of Work.
outbox and inbox behavior.
idempotency.
transaction and rollback behavior.

gates.title: Ingestion and Workflow Foundation Ready
gates.normalized_publication_statement: IKG3 remains not passed until every approved criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: Trigger → Workflow → Stage → Job → Attempt durability.
in-process runner.
retry and failure behavior.
configuration pinning.
receipt, validation, acceptance, and propagation separation.
read-only connector behavior.
authorization enforcement.
workflow observability.

gates.title: First Vertical Slice Verified
gates.normalized_publication_statement: IKG4 remains not passed until every approved criterion is evidenced and approved.
public_pass_criteria: acquire approved read-only approved domain-state inputs.
preserve source payloads.
validate and accept data separately.
construct canonical league state.
preserve exact provenance.
identify versioned state changes.
retain historical interpretation.
expose authorized state and workflow status.
operate idempotently.
explain errors and rejected inputs.
complete the slice using synthetic public fixtures.

research_spikes.title: ChatGPT and Remote MCP Production Compatibility
research_spikes.normalized_publication_statement: Before remote assistant integration is claimed, verify authorized-account compatibility, supported transport and identity behavior, governed read and write interaction, actor attribution, isolation, review integration, and fallback-interface continuity.
public_exit_criteria: Authorized-account and entitlement compatibility is verified.
Transport, identity, governed interaction, attribution, isolation, and review behavior conform.
Fallback interfaces remain authenticated and governed.
